expertDecision.nextAction: finish plugin-coding-tools grep/glob parity decisions, then first-class edit/patch parity, before adding packages/ui trace renderers
expertDecision.rationale: Capability routing is already partially implemented for plugin-coding-tools. The remaining direct local filesystem mechanics are a permission, trace, and behavior-drift risk. Reusable trace components should follow once execution boundaries are coherent.

pluginCodingToolsFsParity.currentPluginBehavior: Session cwd fallback, sandbox path validation, ignore globs, directory-first ordering, file/dir/symlink display, and a 1000-entry cap.
pluginCodingToolsFsParity.currentRemoteParity: fs.list has root guard, hidden/generated exclusions, limits, typed FileStat entries, ignore patterns, and truncation metadata.
pluginCodingToolsFsParity.decision: Routed through capability-router.fs.list with plugin-owned cwd fallback, sandbox validation, directory-first formatting, and local fallback when the router is absent or explicitly unavailable.

pluginCodingToolsFsParity.currentPluginBehavior: Ripgrep-backed regex search with output modes, glob/type filters, context flags, case-insensitive and multiline options, head limit, and VCS excludes.
pluginCodingToolsFsParity.currentRemoteParity: fs.search is literal case-insensitive substring search with path/root/limit/includeHidden only.
pluginCodingToolsFsParity.decision: Do not route yet. Add a first-class search method with these semantics or keep plugin-owned search until parity exists.

pluginCodingToolsFsParity.currentPluginBehavior: File globbing with generated/VCS excludes, recent-file ordering by mtime, and a 100-result cap.
pluginCodingToolsFsParity.currentRemoteParity: No direct glob or recursive find method with pattern semantics.
pluginCodingToolsFsParity.decision: Do not route yet. Add a first-class glob/find method before routing.

pluginCodingToolsFsParity.currentPluginBehavior: Must-read-first and stale-read gate, exact substring replacement, optional replace-all, ambiguity protection, secret detection, and first-line reporting.
pluginCodingToolsFsParity.currentRemoteParity: fs.writeText overwrites full text only and has no compare-and-swap, old-string match, patch, secret gate, or read-state contract.
pluginCodingToolsFsParity.decision: Do not route through generic write. Add a first-class edit/patch method or keep local implementation.

entries.notes: FILE list/read/write/edit/search remains the coding tool semantic surface. Desktop file listing, content reads, and writes prefer the capability router and fall back to the existing sandboxed path when no router is registered or FS is explicitly unavailable.

SHELL remains the agent action. Desktop command execution prefers eliza.pty through the capability router and falls back to the existing local shell path when no router is registered or PTY is explicitly unavailable.

WORKTREE remains the semantic coding action. git worktree add/remove now prefer eliza.git through the capability router and fall back to the existing local Git path when no router is registered or Git is explicitly unavailable.
Auth/config file storage is implementation. Keep Codex CLI semantics in the plugin and route desktop file access through the router after coding-tools.
CLI process execution should route through eliza.pty when running inside the desktop shell.
Command semantics can remain plugin-owned, but host command execution belongs behind eliza.pty.
GitHub API ownership stays in the connector plugin.
Local repository status, diff, and worktree operations should route through eliza.git. Remote GitHub API calls remain plugin-owned.
Document/RAG semantics stay in the app plugin.
Local file reads and searches should route through eliza.fs when running in the desktop shell.
The actual model runtime/provider layer remains plugin-owned.
Desktop status, activation, download, and routing control should coordinate through eliza.local-model while the provider runtime remains in the plugin.
Talk mode remains the semantic voice participant. eliza.voice observes and coordinates rather than replacing it.
Screen capture action semantics may stay plugin-owned, but desktop capture implementation likely belongs behind a future eliza.computer if overlap is confirmed.
System command execution should collapse into eliza.pty, but owner review is needed before changing native-system semantics.
Desktop host/window implementation is a future eliza.computer candidate. Semantic desktop actions should not be deleted without owner review.
Computer-use action semantics remain plugin-owned. Capture, input, window, and clipboard implementation may collapse behind a future eliza.computer.
Browser bridge semantics remain plugin-owned. Desktop browser/window implementation overlap should be audited before any eliza.computer work.
Companion packaging and workspace artifact reads should use eliza.fs in desktop contexts after core coding paths are routed.
